attacks.name: Sun-Disc Club
attacks.detail: Melee Weapon Attack. Hit: 1d8 + STR modifier bludgeoning damage. The Fist of the First Land.

attacks.name: Natural Claws
attacks.detail: Melee Weapon Attack (unarmed strike). Hit: 1d4 + STR modifier slashing damage.

rage.label: Rage
rage.description: +2 damage · BPS Resistance · Advantage on STR checks & saves · 1 minute

features.name: Rage
features.description: Bonus action. +2 damage on STR attacks. Resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. Advantage on STR checks and saves. Lasts 1 minute. 2 uses / long rest.

features.name: Unarmored Defense
features.description: AC = 10 + DEX mod (+2) + CON mod (+2) = 14 while not wearing armor.

features.name: Daunting Roar
features.description: Bonus action. Each creature of your choice within 10 ft must succeed on a WIS save (DC 8 + Prof + CON mod = 12) or become frightened until the end of your next turn. 1 use / short rest.

features.name: Darkvision
features.description: See in dim light as if bright light, and in darkness as if dim light, within 60 feet.

features.name: Wanderer
features.description: Excellent memory for maps and geography. Can find food and fresh water for yourself and up to 5 companions per day in natural environments.

features.name: Subclass — Zealot (Planned)
features.description: Rage is holy fury. Damage feels like divine judgment. Hard to kill — destiny-driven. Sekhmet doesn't rage because he is emotional. He rages because something sacred has been violated. The Zealot path treats survival itself as a divine mandate.

traits.personality: He watches over companions as if they were a litter of newborn cubs. He does not show it openly — but he positions himself between danger and the weak without thinking.
traits.ideal: Glory is remembrance. To be remembered is to resist erasure. Every battle must mean something — his name must echo in story long after his bones bleach in the sand.
traits.bond: He walks so the memory of the First Land does not die. The weight of a fallen pride lives in every step.
traits.flaw: He is slow to trust outsiders. Respect must be earned in sweat and blood. You do not simply enter a pride by standing near it.

carried.name: Sun-Disc Club
carried.description: Greatclub — The Fist of the First Land

carried.name: First-Land Stone Fragment
carried.description: A petrified shard of his homeland

carried.name: Gold Ceremonial Arm Cuff
carried.description: Marks victories of the First Land

backstory.summary: Before the Temporal Compression, his homeland breathed with mythic air. The horizon was wider. Spirits walked the surface of things. Then the world hardened. Matter thickened. What moved like wind became stone-bound. His pride faded — some into legend, some into the petrified ruins that scholars now mistake for ancient monuments. He held form. Not because he was strongest — but because his will refused to dissolve. He walks a world smaller than it should be, carrying the memory of everything that was lost when the mythic gave way to the physical.

attacks.name: Fire Bolt
attacks.notes: Ranged spell attack. Range 120 ft. Cantrip.

attacks.name: Chill Touch
attacks.notes: Range 120 ft. Target can't regain HP until start of your next turn. Cantrip.

attacks.name: Ray of Frost
attacks.notes: Range 60 ft. Target speed reduced by 10 ft until start of your next turn. Cantrip.

attacks.name: Thunderclap
attacks.notes: CON Save DC 11. Each creature within 5 ft. Cantrip.

attacks.name: Druidcraft
attacks.notes: Cantrip. Simple nature magic — predict weather, light/snuff small flames, harmless effects.

arcane_recovery.label: Arcane Recovery
arcane_recovery.description: Recover spell slots totaling ≤ 1 (half wizard level rounded up). Cannot recover 6th level or higher.

features.name: Arcane Recovery
features.description: Once per day on a Short Rest, recover spell slots totaling ≤ half wizard level (rounded up). At Level 1: recover one 1st-level slot. Cannot recover 6th level or higher.

features.name: Wizard Spellbook
features.description: Contains 6 prepared 1st-level spells. Gain 2 new spells per wizard level. Copy from scrolls and tomes (2 hrs + 50 gp/spell level). Spellbook is a pressed-flower diary that also happens to contain the secrets of arcane reality.

traits.personality: Small in stature, enormous in presence. Her curiosity is insatiable — she will touch everything, ask about everything, remember everything. Her laughter is genuine; her silences are calculating.
traits.ideal: Knowledge without application is just hoarding. She studies the boundary between life and death not out of morbidity, but because she believes something important lives there — something the world is pretending not to notice.
traits.bond: Her spellbook contains pressed flowers, margin notes in three languages, and a spell she hasn't been able to cast yet. The people she travels with have become, unexpectedly, something she would cross swamps for.
traits.flaw: She occasionally forgets that other people cannot fly, hide instantly, or talk to mushrooms — which leads to plans that only work if you're her. She also underestimates her own size in situations requiring physical presence.

backstory.summary: She arrived at Witherbloom with pressed flowers already in her spellbook and a question she hadn't told anyone yet. The swamp suited her — it didn't apologize for being complicated, for smelling of both rot and bloom at once. It studied the same question she did: where exactly does life end and death begin, and is anything actually living in that gap? She is very small. She compensates with extraordinary thoroughness. Her notes are annotated. Her annotations have annotations. Her spellbook has a table of contents and an index.

attacks.name: Dagger
attacks.detail: Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack (range 20/60). Finesse, light, thrown.

attacks.name: Acid Splash
attacks.detail: DEX Save DC 13. One or two creatures within 5 ft of each other. Range 60 ft.

attacks.name: Ray of Frost
attacks.detail: Ranged Spell Attack. Range 60 ft. Target speed reduced by 10 ft until start of your next turn.

attacks.name: Fire Bolt
attacks.detail: Ranged Spell Attack. Range 120 ft. Can ignite flammable objects.

attacks.name: Shocking Grasp
attacks.detail: Melee Spell Attack. Advantage on attack if target is wearing metal armor. Target can't take reactions until start of its next turn.

sorcery_points.label: Sorcery Points
sorcery_points.description: Flexible Casting and Metamagic fuel. Gained at level 2, but tracked here.

features.name: Draconic Resilience
features.description: HP max increases by 1 per sorcerer level. When not wearing armor, AC = 13 + DEX modifier (currently 15, but using 12 due to equipment choice).

features.name: Draconic Ancestor: Black Dragon
features.description: You can speak, read, and write Draconic. Whenever you make a CHA check when interacting with dragons, your proficiency bonus is doubled if it applies. Damage type associated with ancestor: Acid.

features.name: Deathless Nature
features.description: You have escaped death. You have advantage on saving throws against disease and being poisoned, and you have resistance to poison damage. You don't need to eat, drink, breathe, or sleep. You finish a long rest in 4 hours if you spend it in an inactive, motionless state.

features.name: Knowledge from a Past Life
features.description: You can add 1d6 to an ability check using a skill you're not proficient in. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus (+2). You regain all expended uses on a long rest.

features.name: Heart of Darkness
features.description: Those who look into your eyes can see you have faced unimaginable horror and are not easily broken. Common folk go out of their way to help you, though they may be fearful. They will offer shelter, aid, and assistance—not out of kindness, but out of fear of what might happen if they don't.

features.name: Harrowing Event
features.description: Prior to becoming a Reborn, you witnessed something that still haunts you. You escaped, but others weren't so lucky. The memory consumes you, and you've dedicated yourself to understanding what happened—and preventing it from happening again.

traits.personality: She speaks in riddles and metaphors, not because she's trying to be cryptic, but because literal language feels too small for what she's trying to express. She's patient with those willing to think, and dismissive of those who prefer easy answers.
traits.ideal: Knowledge is survival. The world hides its rules behind assumptions and tradition. She will find the truth underneath—no matter how uncomfortable it makes people.
traits.bond: She carries a black feather that never decays. It's a fragment of the moment she died—and the moment she came back. She doesn't know what it means yet, but it hums when magic is near.
traits.flaw: She has difficulty connecting with others emotionally. Death rewrote something fundamental in her—she can't feel fear the way she used to, and joy feels muted. She envies those who still burn with life.

carried.name: Arcane Focus (Crystal)
carried.description: Black crystal that reflects no light

carried.name: Dungeoneer's Pack
carried.description: Includes backpack, crowbar, hammer, pitons, torches, tinderbox, rations, waterskin, rope

carried.name: Monster Hunter's Pack
carried.description: Chest, crowbar, hammer, stakes, holy symbol, holy water, manacles, mirror, oil, tinderbox, torches

carried.name: Black Feather (Trinket)
carried.description: Never decays. Hums near magic. Fragment of death and rebirth.

backstory.summary: She died. That much she remembers—the cold, the dark, the way sound fell away like water draining from a basin. Then she woke. Not in a grave. Not in a temple. In a ruin older than any map she's seen since. Something brought her back, but it wasn't kindness. It was purpose. She doesn't know what that purpose is yet, but she can feel it in her bones—a low hum, a direction without a destination. She speaks to the dead more easily than the living now. Not because she's morbid, but because they're honest. The living lie. The living forget. The dead remember everything, even if they no longer care. She's searching for the thing that pulled her back across the threshold. Not to thank it. Not to destroy it. To understand it. Because if death isn't the end, then the world is operating under rules no one bothered to write down—and she will not be caught unprepared again.
